summary: Added external decompilation tooling as git subtrees: m2c under tools/m2c, m2ctx adapted for the project's context-generation needs, and asm-differ with a patch by BR-. The stated goal was to make future tooling able to interface with m2c's AST directly and to provide a context generation pipeline that works after a fresh clone. The PR also cleaned up subtree history by squashing after a force-push.

key_files.role: Subtree import of matt-kempster/m2c at commit 50a2858259b404469e513f1bd15e345b1d1be1f9, intended to support direct interaction with m2c's AST.
Subtree import of ethteck/m2ctx at commit 925f990383fea12f29269afdf177221a4d06dc58, adapted for Melee context generation.

Subtree import of simonlindholm/asm-differ at commit ec8f731c99b2cc80dd896d8179872104b1ee8cba with a patch credited to BR-; exact path inferred from the subtree/tooling title because the PR slice has no changed-file list.

what_changed: Imported m2c as a git subtree, specifically matt-kempster/m2c commit 50a2858259b404469e513f1bd15e345b1d1be1f9.
Placed the m2c subtree in tools/m2c.
Imported m2ctx as a git subtree from ethteck/m2ctx commit 925f990383fea12f29269afdf177221a4d06dc58.
Adapted m2ctx to project needs, with the caveat that it worked under MSYS2 python3 but not native Windows Python.
Imported asm-differ as a git subtree from simonlindholm/asm-differ commit ec8f731c99b2cc80dd896d8179872104b1ee8cba.
Included an asm-differ patch credited to BR-.
Force-pushed to clean up git history by squashing subtree history.
smart_moves: Vendored decompilation tools as git subtrees rather than relying on ad-hoc local installs, improving reproducibility for contributors after a fresh clone.
Chose explicit upstream commits for each imported tool, making the tooling state auditable and repeatable.
Separated imported tools into subtree locations, preserving a clear boundary between project source and external tooling.
Called out platform behavior for m2ctx: works under MSYS2 python3 but not native Windows Python, which is useful for future contributor setup/debugging.
Squashed subtree history before merge to avoid excessively noisy repository history from vendor imports.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects, context generation and assembly diffing should be treated as first-class repository tooling so new contributors can run the matching workflow from a fresh clone.
When importing external decomp tools, pin exact upstream commits in the PR body so later maintainers can compare local modifications against upstream.
Git subtrees can be a practical way to vendor tools like m2c, m2ctx, and asm-differ while retaining some upstream provenance.
Document Python/environment limitations at import time; platform mismatches can block context generation even when the tool is present.
If subtree import diffs are huge, history cleanup/squashing is an important maintenance step.
naming_conventions: External tooling directories are named after the upstream tools: m2c, m2ctx, and asm-differ.
m2c was explicitly located at tools/m2c.
The PR title uses backticked tool names and hyphenated asm-differ, matching the upstream project name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adding asm-differ supports local comparison of generated assembly against target assembly for matching work.
Adding m2c supports decompiler-assisted translation and future tooling that can interface directly with m2c's AST.
Adding m2ctx supports a context-generation pipeline for decompilation work, intended to be runnable after a fresh clone.
The PR slice does not include exact command-line usage or code-level matching tactics beyond the imported tool purposes.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Author follow-up noted that m2ctx was added as another subtree to make a working context-generation pipeline available after a fresh clone.
Author follow-up noted a force-push to significantly clean up git history by squashing subtrees.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for tooling that interfaces directly with the m2c AST.
Check later PRs for m2ctx fixes or native Windows Python compatibility.
Check whether asm-differ subtree updates preserve or replace the BR- patch.
Search for scripts or documentation added after this PR that invoke tools/m2c, m2ctx, or asm-differ.
Verify exact asm-differ subtree path in the full repository history, since the PR slice did not include changed files.
